title: Add warning when initialization instead of reset used for flip-flops

features.name: reg_inline_initialization_in_sequential_logic
features.description: Code should include a register variable declared with inline initialization (e.g., reg b = 1;) that is used in a clocked always @(posedge) block without a corresponding reset condition. This pattern relies on simulation initialization rather than explicit reset, which is not ASIC-friendly design practice.
features.snippet:
reg b = 1; // inline initialization
always @(posedge clk)
    b <= ~b; // no reset path

features.name: sequential_logic_missing_reset_path
features.description: Code should include a sequential logic block (always @(posedge clk)) that updates a register but lacks an explicit reset condition. ASIC designs should use synchronous or asynchronous reset rather than relying on initialization values to ensure predictable behavior after power-on.
features.snippet:
always @(posedge clk)
    a <= ~a; // no reset path
